areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 程序 · Harman 承诺

name: Florou & Florou

one.zh: 程序问题——丈夫申请解除 Harman 默示承诺，以便将家事诉讼中的宣誓证据用于维多利亚州最高法院的诉讼；法院认定未构成「特殊情形」，驳回申请。
one.en: Practice & procedure — the husband sought release from the Harman undertaking to use family-law affidavit evidence in Supreme Court of Victoria proceedings; the court found no 'special circumstances' and dismissed the application.

issue.zh: 丈夫能否证明存在「特殊情形」，从而解除他对本院的默示（Harman）承诺与 s 114Q、规则 6.04 的限制，以便把本案两份宣誓书用于最高法院的移交申请？
issue.en: Has the husband shown 'special circumstances' to be released from the implied (Harman) undertaking and the restrictions in s 114Q and r 6.04, so as to use two affidavits filed here in his Supreme Court transfer application?

catch: FAMILY LAW — Practice and procedure – Application for release from Harman undertaking – Where the husband seeks leave to use affidavit evidence filed in proceedings under the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) for the purpose of proceedings in the Supreme Court of Victoria – Where the affidavits sought to be used traverse personal and sensitive information of the parties and their adult children – Consideration of authorities on special circumstances – Special circumstances not established – Insufficient nexus between the proceedings – Leave refused

subseq.zh: 本案为终局裁定（驳回申请）；费用问题留待书面裁定。本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: This is a final ruling (application dismissed); costs reserved for determination on the papers. No further record in this library.

areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 财产（临时）· 配偶赡养

name: Van & Aixin

one.zh: 财产（临时）——妻子申请配偶赡养与诉讼资金；双方婚内生活奢华，但妻子未能证明其无力充分自养，赡养申请被拒；法院按「一比一」对等方式作出有期限的诉讼资助令。
one.en: Property (interim) — the wife sought spousal maintenance and litigation funding; the parties had an extravagant marital lifestyle but the wife did not establish an incapacity to support herself adequately, so maintenance was refused; a time-limited dollar-for-dollar litigation-funding order was made.

issue.zh: 临时阶段：妻子是否证明其「无力充分自养」以获得配偶赡养（s 72/s 75）？以及在丈夫可经家族取得资金的情况下，是否应作出诉讼资助令（s 114UB）？
issue.en: On an interim basis: has the wife established she is 'unable to support herself adequately' for spousal maintenance (s 72/s 75)? And, given the husband's access to family funds, should a litigation-funding order be made (s 114UB)?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PROPERTY – Interim orders – Where the wife seeks orders for spousal maintenance and litigation funding – Where the parties enjoyed an extravagant lifestyle during the marriage – Where the wife has not established an incapacity to support herself adequately – Where the significant dispute in the substantive proceedings relate to the ownership of companies – Where, on any case, the husband has access to significant funds through the paternal grandparents – Litigation funding order made on a dollar-for-dollar basis for a limited duration.

subseq.zh: 本案为临时裁定；实体财产分割、第三方实体异议（定于 2026-06-15）等留待后续。本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: Interim ruling; the substantive property case and third-party entity objections (listed 15 Jun 2026) remain. No further record in this library.

areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 笔误更正规则 · 证据 · 费用
areaTag.en: Family law · slip rule · evidence · costs

name: Niek & Oddrun (No 2)

one.zh: 笔误更正规则（slip rule）——更正一项按固定金额分割「受控资金账户」、却遗漏了其后所生利息的终局财产令，使其符合法院 62.5/37.5 的本意；并就费用准予使用调解中编制的资产负债表。
one.en: Slip rule — correcting a final property order that split a Controlled Money Account by fixed amounts but omitted the interest it later accrued, to reflect the court's intended 62.5/37.5 division; and on costs, allowing use of a balance sheet prepared at mediation.

issue.zh: 法院能否依「笔误更正规则」（规则 10.13(e)——命令不反映法院本意）更正终局财产令，使妻获得受控资金账户所生利息的应得份额？调解中共同编制的资产负债表能否就费用问题举证？应否判夫承担妻的费用？
issue.en: Can the court use the 'slip rule' (r 10.13(e) — order not reflecting the court's intention) to correct the final orders so the wife receives her share of interest on a Controlled Money Account? Can a balance sheet jointly prepared at mediation be adduced on costs? And should the husband pay the wife's costs?

catch: FAMILY LAW – SLIP RULE – Where the final property orders did not make provision for the wife to receive interest accrued on monies held in a Controlled Money Account – Where the orders as pronounced did not reflect the intention of the presiding judicial officer – Where the order is set aside and replaced. FAMILY LAW – EVIDENCE – Mediation agreement provided for confidentiality – Whether a Balance Sheet jointly prepared during a mediation can be adduced on questions of costs. FAMILY LAW – COSTS – Consideration of s 114UB – Where the Court is satisfied there are justifying circumstances to make an order for costs.

subseq.zh: 本案为同案系列第 2 号判决（实体终局判决见 Niek & Oddrun [2025] FedCFamC1F 802）。本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: This is the second decision in the series (the substantive final judgment is Niek & Oddrun [2025] FedCFamC1F 802). No further record in this library.

areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 子女 · 临时 · 药检

name: Eide & Yoxall (No 7)

one.zh: 养育（临时）——子女自 2020 年 12 月分居以来一直随母生活、仅在监督下见父；西澳儿童保护部门将子女带离母亲照顾。父亲的程序申请被驳回。
one.en: Parenting (interim) — the child had lived with the mother and saw the father only on supervised time since separation in December 2020; the child was removed from the mother's care by the WA child-protection authority. The father's application was dismissed.

issue.zh: 在一宗漫长的临时养育诉讼中，父亲（把诉求收窄后）申请命令母亲接受毛囊药检；法院是否应依 s 102NJ(1)(c) 作出该命令？以及随之而来的费用应如何处理？
issue.en: In long-running interim parenting litigation the father (having narrowed his application) sought an order that the mother undergo hair-follicle drug testing; should the court make that order under s 102NJ(1)(c)? And how should costs be dealt with?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – INTERIM – Where the child has lived with the mother and spent only supervised time with the father since the parties' separation in December 2020 – Where the child was removed from the mother's care by the Western Australian child protection authority.

subseq.zh: 本案为系列第 7 号判决；本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: This is the seventh decision in the series; no further record in this library.

path: cases/FedCFamC1F/2026/[2026] FedCFamC1F 250 Department of Families, Seniors, Disability Services and Child Safety & Pensak.json
name: Department of Families, Seniors, Disability Services and Child Safety & Pensak

one.zh: 海牙公约:子女惯常居所在日本、不法滞留澳洲且无例外成立,法院命令两名(原住民)子女返回日本由当地法院裁断未来。
one.en: Hague Convention: with the children habitually resident in Japan, wrongfully retained in Australia and no exception made out, the court ordered the two (Indigenous) children returned to Japan for its courts to decide their future.

issue.zh: 两名子女被滞留澳洲前的惯常居所是否在日本?父亲主张的原住民文化联系等是否构成拒绝返还的「重大风险/无法忍受处境」例外?
issue.en: Were the children habitually resident in Japan before being retained in Australia, and do the father's claims (Aboriginal cultural connection and others) make out the 'grave risk / intolerable situation' exception to return?

catch: CHILD ABDUCTION – Hague Convention – Application for return of children to Japan – Determination of habitual residence – Children residing in Japan for extended period – Ambiguous parental intention – Integration into Japanese family, educational and social environment – Habitual residence established in Japan – No regulatory exceptions made out – Return order made.

subseq.zh: 返还命令不等于最佳利益终判;子女返日后,有关其照料与未来的实体问题由日本法院裁断。
subseq.en: A return order is not a final best-interests decision; once back in Japan, the substantive questions about the children's care and future fall to the Japanese courts.

name: Houtem & Houtem

one.zh: 拒绝分项审理:法院认为单独先审 5 项(或前两项)争点不会显著省时省钱或缩窄争点,反生卫星上诉与不一致风险,命一次性整体审理。
one.en: Separate-issues hearing refused: the court was not satisfied that hiving off five (or the first two) issues would significantly save cost or narrow matters, and saw risks of satellite appeals and inconsistent findings — all issues to be tried together.

issue.zh: 在涉及推翻 2023 年同意命令、撤销 s 90C 财务协议、及是否存在事实婚关系等多重争点的复杂诉讼中,应否依 r 10.10 先就部分争点分项审理?
issue.en: In complex litigation seeking to set aside 2023 consent orders, set aside a s 90C financial agreement, and determine a de facto relationship, should some issues be heard separately under r 10.10?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Oral application for a separate issues hearing pursuant to r 10.10 of the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Family Law) Rules 2021 (Cth) (“the Rules”) – Where the applicant opposes the oral application – Where the parties have been engaged in two sets of prior proceedings as to parenting and property, each being compromised by way of consent orders made in 2015 and 2023 – Where the parties entered a s 90C Agreement as to spousal maintenance contemporaneously with the making of the 2023 consent orders – Where the parties had a third child in 2025 subsequent to an Order for Divorce taking effect – Where the respondent seeks a separate determination of five specified issues including as to whether the parties third child is a child of a marriage for the purposes of s 60F of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) ("the Act") – Consideration of s 95 of the Act and r 1.04 of the Rules – Where the respondent's submission that there would be a significant saving of costs or substantial narrowing of issues to dispose of the proceedings in a timely manner is not accepted – Where it is just and convenient for all questions of fact and law to be determined in a single trial event – Oral application dismissed – Costs reserved.

subseq.zh: 分项审理申请被驳回,相关费用予以保留;全部事实与法律争点将于单次审理一并裁断。
subseq.en: The application was dismissed and costs reserved; all questions of fact and law are to be determined at a single trial.

areaTag.zh: 家事法 · 程序 · 上诉期间暂缓
areaTag.en: Family law · procedure · stay pending appeal

name: Steinar & Steinar (No 2)

one.zh: 程序问题——母亲就终局养育令提出上诉，并申请在上诉期间暂缓执行该命令；父亲与独立儿童律师反对。法院认定暂缓不符合子女最佳利益、且上诉不会因此落空，驳回申请。
one.en: Practice & procedure — the mother appealed final parenting orders and applied to stay them pending the appeal; the father and the Independent Children's Lawyer opposed. The court found a stay was not in the child's best interests and the appeal would not be rendered nugatory, and dismissed the application.

issue.zh: 在母亲已就终局养育令提出上诉的情况下，法院是否应在上诉裁决前暂缓执行这些命令（依规则 13.12 与 Aldridge & Keaton 的暂缓原则，并以子女最佳利益为重要考量）？
issue.en: Where the mother has appealed the final parenting orders, should the court stay them pending the appeal (under r 13.12 and the Aldridge & Keaton stay principles, with the child's best interests a significant consideration)?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Stay application – Where the mother sought a stay of final parenting orders – Where the mother had filed a Notice of Appeal in respect of those final parenting orders – Where the father and the Independent Children's Lawyer opposed the stay application – Where a stay of the final parenting orders would not be in the child's best interests – Where the mother's pending appeal would not be rendered nugatory if the stay is not granted – Application dismissed.

subseq.zh: 本案为同案系列第 2 号判决；上诉定于 2026-07-02 由全席法院审理。本资料库未见进一步记录。
subseq.en: This is the second decision in the series; the appeal was listed before the Full Court on 2 July 2026. No further record in this library.

name: Khuu & Trang

one.zh: 财产保全禁制令获批:丈夫在分居财产诉讼中卖掉豪车、$50万款项汇往海外,法院认定其「公司存货/常规交易」说法不成立,命将该款冻结并存入律师信托账户。
one.en: Preservation injunction granted: in property litigation the husband sold a luxury car and remitted the $500,000 overseas; the court rejected his 'company trading stock / ordinary course' account and froze the sum into a solicitor's trust account.

issue.zh: 在财产分割诉讼中,丈夫处置豪车并将 $50万售款汇往海外,妻子可否获得禁制令以保全该款、防止资产被进一步耗散?
issue.en: In property-adjustment litigation, where the husband disposed of a luxury car and remitted the $500,000 overseas, can the wife obtain an injunction to preserve that sum against further dissipation?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Where the wife seeks injunctive orders for the preservation of property – Where the husband disposed of a luxury motor vehicle and remitted the proceeds of sale overseas – Where the wife contends the husband was the legal and beneficial owner of the motor vehicle – Where the husband contends the motor vehicle was owned by a company and the sale proceeds were remitted in payment of a liability of the company to a third party within the ordinary course of business – Where the husband had previously disclosed in court documents under oath that he was the legal owner of the motor vehicle – Where the husband failed to provide financial records of the company proving its ownership of the motor vehicle – Where it is not established that the company had a pre-existing liability owed to the third party at least in the amount of the proceeds of sale of the motor vehicle – Where it is not established that the proceeds of sale are not within the husband’s possession or control – Order made directing the husband to cause the proceeds of sale of the motor vehicle to be paid into the wife’s solicitor’s trust account pending further order.

subseq.zh: 禁制令以贴合现状的措辞作出(非全照妻方原请求),款项存入信托账户待进一步命令;费用申请与妻方进一步申请定于 2026-05-08 一并聆讯。
subseq.en: The injunction was made in terms reflecting the found circumstances (not exactly as sought); the sum is held in trust pending further order; the costs application and the wife's further applications were listed together for 8 May 2026.

name: Alinari & Ovidio

one.zh: 无争辩聆讯:母亲长期缺席、有安全隐忧,法院在其缺席下经父亲与独立子女律师同意作出终局命令——5 岁子女随父生活、父亲独有长期决策权,暂不安排与母亲相处。
one.en: Undefended hearing: with the mother long absent and safety concerns, the court made final orders in her absence by consent between the father and the Independent Children's Lawyer — the five-year-old to live with the father, the father to have sole long-term decision-making, and no time with the mother for now.

issue.zh: 在母亲长期不出庭、且有证据显示其精神健康下滑危及子女安全的情况下,可否在其缺席下,经父亲与独立子女律师同意作出终局养育命令?
issue.en: Where the mother repeatedly failed to attend and evidence showed her declining mental health endangered the child's safety, can final parenting orders be made in her absence, by consent between the father and the Independent Children's Lawyer?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Undefended hearing – Where the Father and Independent Children’s Lawyer sought final orders be made in the absence of the Mother – Where there is a history of non-attendance and non-compliance by the Mother – Where the Father and Independent Children’s Lawyer sought orders by consent for the Father to have sole decision making responsibility, for the child to live with him, for the Father to be able to obtain an Australian passport for the child without the consent of the Mother, and for the Father to be able to travel internationally with the child without the consent of the Mother – Where the Court made final orders by agreement as between the Father and the Independent Children’s Lawyer and in the absence of the Mother.

subseq.zh: 命令为终局,但母亲若能合理解释缺席并展示可信主张,仍可申请重启程序;针对母亲的独立子女律师费用申请被拒。
subseq.en: The orders are final, but the mother may apply to re-open if she can reasonably explain her absence and show a plausible case; the ICL's costs application against the mother was declined.

name: Dershowitz & Agate

one.zh: 重新审视终局养育令:父亲请求重启 2024 年的终局养育命令（当时认定他对子女构成不可接受风险）。法院依 s 65DAAA 认定无「重大情势变更」，且父亲未真正处理其风险——驳回申请。
one.en: Reconsidering final parenting orders: the father sought to reopen 2024 final orders (which had found he posed an unacceptable risk to the children). Applying s 65DAAA, the court found no 'significant change of circumstances' and that the father had not addressed the risks — the application was dismissed.

issue.zh: 在 2024 年终局养育命令认定父亲对子女构成不可接受风险后，父亲依 s 65DAAA 请求重启程序，是否存在「重大情势变更」、且重启是否符合子女最佳利益?
issue.en: After 2024 final orders found the father posed an unacceptable risk, has there been a 'significant change of circumstances' under s 65DAAA, and is reconsidering the orders in the children's best interests?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Where the father seeks that final parenting orders made in 2024 be reconsidered – Consideration of s 65DAAA of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) – Where the Court finds that there has not been a significant change in circumstances – Where, despite assertions otherwise, the father has not addressed concerns giving rise to a finding that he posed an unacceptable risk to the children in the first tranche of proceedings – Application dismissed.

subseq.zh: 父亲的申请被驳回，2024 年终局养育命令（母亲独有亲权、子女随母生活、不与父相处并附 s 68B 限制令）维持不变。费用问题另循程序处理，母亲可在 14 日内申请。
subseq.en: The father's application was dismissed; the 2024 final orders (mother's sole parental responsibility, children living with her, no time with the father, with s 68B restraints) stand. Costs are dealt with separately, with the mother able to apply within 14 days.

name: Stephanidi & Lawrence (No 3)

one.zh: 传票异议:独立子女律师向健康机构发出传票调取父母及子女病历;母亲以缺乏正当取证目的、属「受保护私密信息」（s 102BA）为由提出八点异议。法院依 s 102BD(5) 亲自查阅文件后，认定其相关、不触发保护，驳回异议，仅准独立子女律师亲自查阅。
one.en: Subpoena objection: the Independent Children's Lawyer subpoenaed the parents' and children's health records; the mother objected on eight grounds amounting to lack of legitimate forensic purpose and protected confidences (s 102BA). After inspecting the documents itself under s 102BD(5), the court found them relevant, not protected, dismissed the objection, and allowed inspection by the ICL only.

issue.zh: 独立子女律师向健康机构调取父母与子女病历的传票，是否因缺乏正当取证目的、或落入「受保护私密信息」而应被异议成功阻止?
issue.en: Should a subpoena by the Independent Children's Lawyer for the parents' and children's health records be defeated by objection on grounds of lack of legitimate forensic purpose or that the material is a 'protected confidence'?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Subpoena Objection – Where the Independent Children's Lawyer filed a subpoena to the O Health District for updating material in relation to the parents and childrens health records – Where the Respondent filed a Notice of Objection to the subpoena raising eight (8) grounds of objection which broadly amount to an assertion of a lack of legitimate forensic purpose and the material falls within a protected confidences pursuant to s 102BA of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) – Where the Court heard oral submissions from the parties – Where the Court inspected the material pursuant to s 102BD(5) of the Act – Where the Court found that the material produced was relevant and did not warrant any direction being made pursuant to s102BD of the Act.

subseq.zh: 异议被驳回，仅准独立子女律师亲自查阅（不得复印）。法院保留在情况变化时（如父母日后聘请律师）放宽查阅限制的可能。实体程序仍部分待审，相关上诉已聆讯但尚未裁决。
subseq.en: The objection was dismissed; only the ICL may inspect, in person (no photocopying). The court left open relaxing that restriction if circumstances change (e.g. if the parents obtain representation). The substantive proceedings remain part-heard, with an appeal heard but not yet determined.

name: Beiler & Jaskolski (No 4)

one.zh: CONTRAVENTION – PARENTING – Where the Father alleges that, since the making of the final orders on 5 September 2025, the Mother has not complied with …
one.en: CONTRAVENTION – PARENTING – Where the Father alleges that, since the making of the final orders on 5 September 2025, the Mother has not complied with …

catch: FAMILY LAW – CONTRAVENTION – PARENTING – Where the Father alleges that, since the making of the final orders on 5 September 2025, the Mother has not complied with the “spend time” orders as sought by her – Where the Mother’s case was that she had not contravened the orders or, in the alternative, that she had a reasonable excuse for not complying with the orders – Where children (aged 14 and 10) have now not spent time with the Father in accordance with orders since March 2025 – Where Court is satisfied the Mother intentionally failed to comply with the relevant orders – Where no reasonable excuse established – Where Mother’s evidence as to the children’s reluctance to spend time with the Father is insufficiently particularised – Where the Mother gave evidence that she relies upon the views of the older child in relation to whether that child and the younger child should be made available for time with the Father – Where declaration made that the Mother contravened the orders without reasonable excuse – Where the Mother is required to enter into a two-year bond without surety or security on condition that she complies with the orders in respect of the 10 year old child –Where the Court is not satisfied that make-up time is in the children’s best interests.

subseq.zh: 标准层(自动摘要)。完整精读可后续升级。
subseq.en: Standard tier (auto-summary). May be upgraded to a full deep-dive later.

name: Apostolova & Nuallain (No 4)

one.zh: PARENTING – Live with and spend time with arrangements – Decision-making responsibility – Orders for the children to live with the mother and spend ti…
one.en: PARENTING – Live with and spend time with arrangements – Decision-making responsibility – Orders for the children to live with the mother and spend ti…

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Live with and spend time with arrangements – Decision-making responsibility – Orders for the children to live with the mother and spend time with the father from Thursday after school to Tuesday before school each alternate week and half of school holidays.

subseq.zh: 标准层(自动摘要)。完整精读可后续升级。
subseq.en: Standard tier (auto-summary). May be upgraded to a full deep-dive later.

name: Manco & Boels

one.zh: 养育·原住民子女:三岁原住民女童随（非原住民）祖母生活，已约 18 个月未见母亲。法院认定祖母对母亲及其家族的诸多指控不可靠、风险可经监督化解，命母亲在联络中心起步、逐步重建关系（含让孩子接触其原住民文化），父亲在完成育儿课程后逐步增加相处，祖母在协商程序下作长期决策。
one.en: Parenting · an Aboriginal child: a three-year-old Aboriginal girl living with her (non-Indigenous) paternal grandmother had not seen her mother for about 18 months. The court found the grandmother's many allegations against the mother and her family unreliable and the risks able to be managed by supervision, and ordered the mother to re-establish a relationship (first at a contact centre, building the child's connection to her Aboriginal culture), the father to have graduating time after completing parenting courses, and the grandmother to make long-term decisions through a consultation process.

issue.zh: 在三岁原住民子女已与（非原住民）祖母同住、长期未见母亲的情况下:长期决策权应如何在父母与祖母间分配?对母亲、父亲所称的风险是否「不可接受」、能否经监督化解?以及如何落实原住民子女享有其文化的权利?
issue.en: Where a three-year-old Aboriginal child lives with her (non-Indigenous) grandmother and has long been apart from her mother: how should long-term decision-making be allocated among the parents and the grandmother; are the asserted risks 'unacceptable' or able to be managed by supervision; and how is the Aboriginal child's right to enjoy her culture to be given effect?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Final hearing regarding a child of Aboriginal descent – Where the child's mother is Aboriginal and does not currently have a relationship with the child – Where the child presently lives with the paternal grandmother and where this arrangement is to continue – Where the father currently spends limited time with the child supervised by the paternal grandmother – Where the paternal grandmother is highly critical of the mother and her family – Where the mother and the father pose some risks, of varying degrees and natures, in spending time with the child but which the court finds can be ameliorated – Orders made for the father to spend time with the child on a graduating basis and subject to the completion of named parenting courses – Orders made for the mother to re-establish a relationship with the child, first at a contact centre, and thereafter with the supervision of the mother's support worker – Orders made for the paternal grandmother to make major long-term decisions for the child subject to a process of consultation – Where a relationship with the mother provides the child with the best opportunity to learn about and experience her Aboriginal culture and identity.

subseq.zh: 所有先前命令撤销。父母与祖母均有亲权;祖母对重大长期事项有独有决策权，但须经先通知、收集父母意见的协商程序。子女随祖母生活;母亲先在联络中心监督相处不少于 10 次，其后由 NDIS 个案工作者监督;父亲完成指定课程后逐步增加相处至过夜。三方平摊联络中心费用。法院不鼓励另启新程序（除 s 65DAAA 既有途径外）。
subseq.en: All previous orders discharged. The parents and the grandmother all have parental responsibility; the grandmother has sole decision-making on major long-term issues, but through a consultation process (prior notice and the parents' views). The child lives with the grandmother; the mother first has at least 10 supervised visits at a contact centre, then supervision by an NDIS case worker; the father's time graduates to overnights after completing named courses. The three share the contact-centre costs equally. The court did not encourage fresh proceedings beyond the existing s 65DAAA avenue.

name: Pryor & Pryor (No 3)

one.zh: 费用申请:终局财产程序后，妻子申请费用。丈夫以「无力支付」抗辩，但其行为失当——未尽财务披露、未遵守程序命令、未接受妻子庭前和解要约。法院依 s 114UB 命其按固定金额 $35,000 支付妻子费用。
one.en: Costs application: after the final property proceedings, the wife applied for costs. The husband resisted on inability to pay, but his conduct was delinquent — non-disclosure, non-compliance with orders, and refusal of the wife's pre-trial settlement offer. The court ordered him to pay the wife's costs fixed at $35,000 under s 114UB.

issue.zh: 在家事案件「各自承担费用」为常态的前提下，一方因财务不披露、不遵守命令、拒绝合理和解要约等失当行为，是否应被命令支付对方费用?其声称无力支付能否成为屏障?
issue.en: Given that parties to family law proceedings ordinarily bear their own costs, should a party whose conduct was delinquent — non-disclosure, non-compliance, and refusal of a reasonable settlement offer — be ordered to pay the other's costs, and can a claim of inability to pay block that?

catch: FAMILY LAW – COSTS – Where the wife makes an application for costs in substantive property proceedings – Where the husband contends his financial circumstances militate against any costs order being made – Where the husband's conduct of the proceedings was delinquent – Where the husband did not accept the wife's offer of settlement before the trial – Orders made – Husband to pay the wife's costs of and incidental to the financial cause in a fixed sum.

subseq.zh: 费用按固定金额 $35,000 判付，并与 2025 年 11 月已作的费用命令并列（不重复计算）。该数额约为妻子 2025 年 7 月以来财产程序实际费用的 70%。
subseq.en: Costs were fixed at $35,000, payable in addition to (and not double-counting) the earlier November 2025 costs order. The figure represents about 70% of the wife's actual costs in the financial cause since July 2025.

name: Faran & Mor

one.zh: 婚姻无效:申请人请求宣告其 2023 年的婚姻无效,因被申请人结婚时仍与前妻有效婚姻关系（重婚）;申请未获反对,法院依《婚姻法》宣告该婚姻自始无效。
one.en: Nullity: the applicant sought a decree that her 2023 marriage was void because the respondent was still lawfully married to his former wife at the time (bigamy); the application was unopposed and the court declared the marriage void under the Marriage Act.

issue.zh: 一方在结婚时仍与他人存在有效婚姻的情况下,后一段婚姻是否自始无效、应否作出婚姻无效宣告?
issue.en: Where one party was still lawfully married to another person at the time of the marriage, is the later marriage void from the outset, such that a decree of nullity should be made?

catch: FAMILY LAW – NULLITY – Where the applicant seeks a decree of nullity – Where the application was not opposed by the respondent – Where the respondent was married to another person at the time of the entry into the marriage with the applicant – Decree of nullity granted.

subseq.zh: 宣告为终局:该 2023 年婚姻自始无效。被申请人此后已于 2025 年末在中国办妥与前妻的离婚;双方各自承担费用,不涉子女。
subseq.en: The declaration is final: the 2023 marriage is void from the outset. The respondent has since obtained a divorce from his former wife in China in late 2025; each party bears their own costs and there are no children.

name: Dayan & Shaul

one.zh: 养育·不可接受风险:父亲请求与五岁女儿相处，但他与孩子从无关系、有严重家暴史（含胁迫性控制、恐吓、死亡威胁），并有脑损伤、多重精神障碍及吸毒。法院认定其构成不可接受风险、监督亦无法化解，命子女不与父亲相处，并作限制令。
one.en: Parenting · unacceptable risk: the father sought time with his five-year-old daughter, but he has no relationship with her, a serious family-violence history (coercive control, intimidation, death threats), an acquired brain injury, multiple mental-health disorders and drug use. The court found he posed an unacceptable risk that supervision could not ameliorate, ordered the child to spend no time with the father, and made injunctions.

issue.zh: 在父亲与五岁女儿从无关系、且有严重家暴史与多重风险因素的情况下，应否准其相处?所认定的风险能否经监督或限制令化解至可接受程度?
issue.en: Where the father has no relationship with his five-year-old daughter and a serious family-violence history with multiple risk factors, should time be ordered, and can the identified risk be ameliorated to an acceptable level by supervision or injunctions?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Unacceptable risk – Where father seeks time with five-year-old child – Where father has no pre-existing relationship with child – Where mother alleges serious family violence including coercive control, intimidation and threats – Where absence of physical violence does not mean absence of risk – Where father suffers from an acquired brain injury and several mental health disorders – Where there is evidence of illicit substance abuse by the father – Where mother alleges father is dangerous and impulsive.

subseq.zh: 经合意:所有先前养育命令撤销，母亲对五岁子女有独有亲权与决策权、子女随母生活。经法院:子女不与父亲相处;对父亲作出限制令（禁止接近、联络、带离、在社媒发布）。独立子女律师解任，其余申请驳回。父亲日后仍可循 s 65DAAA 的既有途径，但法院不鼓励另启新程序。
subseq.en: By consent: all previous parenting orders discharged; the mother has sole parental responsibility and decision-making for the five-year-old, who lives with her. By the court: the child spends no time with the father; injunctions restrain the father (from approaching, communicating, removing the child, or publishing on social media). The ICL was discharged and other applications dismissed. The father retains the existing s 65DAAA avenue, but the court did not encourage fresh proceedings.

name: Michelaki & Michelaki (No 3)

one.zh: 同意命令的执行与解释:双方就 2024 年财产同意命令的多处理解争执（房产租金归属、销售款扣项含 GST/资本利得税、养老金基金「管理费」提取等）。法院驳回丈夫几乎全部诉求，准许妻子两项（返还违规提取的 $18,000 加利息;一笔 GST 付款计入丈夫份额），其余驳回。
one.en: Enforcing and interpreting consent orders: the parties disputed the meaning of 2024 property consent orders (rental income, sale-proceeds deductions including GST and capital-gains tax, and a 'management fee' drawn from the super fund). The court rejected almost all of the husband's claims, granted the wife two (restoration of $18,000 wrongly withdrawn plus interest, and a GST payment to be brought to account against the husband's share), and dismissed the rest.

issue.zh: 在双方就 2024 年财产同意命令的解释与落实各执一词时，哪些扣项可从房产销售款中扣除（GST 是否属命令所指「负担、税费及合理开支」）?未及转让前的租金归谁?一方擅自从养老金基金提取「管理费」是否违令应返还?
issue.en: Where the parties dispute the construction and implementation of 2024 property consent orders, which deductions may come out of sale proceeds (is GST within the orders' 'encumbrances, taxes and reasonable expenses'?), who is entitled to rent before transfer, and was a party's unilateral withdrawal of a 'management fee' from the super fund a breach to be restored?

catch: FAMILY LAW – ENFORCEMENT – Where final property adjustment orders in force – Where parties disagree about the interpretation of the orders – Where the wife's solicitors hold funds pending distribution of them to parties – Where parties disagree about payments to be made from fund.

subseq.zh: 丈夫的进一步修订执行申请几乎全部被驳回;准许:就 2024 年命令第 13(q)(4) 项应扣的（资本利得）税款付予丈夫或按其指示;丈夫 14 日内向养老金基金返还违规提取的 $18,000 加利息 $1,071.55;一笔已付 $60,834 的 GST 计入丈夫 35% 销售款份额。双方均不获费用。
subseq.en: The husband's further amended enforcement application was almost entirely dismissed; granted: the (capital-gains) tax deducted under order 13(q)(4) of the 2024 orders be paid to the husband or at his direction; the husband restore $18,000 wrongly drawn from the super fund plus $1,071.55 interest within 14 days; and a $60,834 GST payment be brought to account in the husband's 35% share. Neither party was awarded costs.

name: Ruslan & Ruslan (No 2)

one.zh: 养老金分割执行:夫妻双方各自申请执行两年前的同意养老金分割命令（二人是其自管养老金基金的受托人兼仅有成员）。法院认定:养老金分割命令的生效时点是实体性而非机械性条款，执行不是重新审视命令的机会;双方申请均被驳回。
one.en: Superannuation enforcement: each spouse applied to enforce a consent superannuation-splitting order made two years earlier (they are the trustees and sole members of their self-managed super fund). The court held the splitting order's operative time was a substantive, not a machinery, term, and enforcement is not a chance to revisit the order — both applications were dismissed.

issue.zh: 在双方就一项两年前的同意养老金分割命令的执行各执一词（争议本金约 $14,850）时，分割命令的「生效时点」属可在执行中调整的机械性条款，还是须经 s 79A 变更的实体性条款?应否准予所求执行救济?
issue.en: Where the spouses dispute the enforcement of a two-year-old consent superannuation-splitting order (the contested sum about $14,850), is the order's 'operative time' a machinery term adjustable on enforcement, or a substantive term requiring variation under s 79A — and should the relief sought be granted?

catch: FAMILY LAW – ENFORCEMENT – Where the parties each apply to enforce a consent superannuation splitting order made more than two years ago – Where the parties are the trustees and sole members of their self-managed superannuation fund – Consideration of the utility of the Court making a second order pursuant to s 106A of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) – Consideration as to whether the operative time of a superannuation splitting order is a substantive order or a machinery order – Where enforcement is not an opportunity to revisit the substance of an order sought to be enforced.

subseq.zh: 双方各自的执行申请均被驳回:丈夫的 s 106A 申请属重复、无效用（2024 年命令已有同等条款，可经登记官执行）;妻子未先以 s 79A 申请变更分割命令的生效时点，且其救济在裁量上「不公平」。费用问题另循程序处理。
subseq.en: Both enforcement applications were dismissed: the husband's s 106A relief was duplicative and of no utility (the 2024 order already contained an equivalent provision, executable via a registrar); the wife had not first sought to vary the splitting order's operative time under s 79A, and her relief was, in discretion, 'inequitable'. Costs are dealt with separately.

name: Janvier & Domas (No 4)

one.zh: PARENTING – Where the husband and wife both seek sole decision making responsibility for major long term issues for their daughter – Where the husband…
one.en: PARENTING – Where the husband and wife both seek sole decision making responsibility for major long term issues for their daughter – Where the husband…

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Where the husband and wife both seek sole decision making responsibility for major long term issues for their daughter – Where the husband seeks a change of the child’s residence from Melbourne to Hobart when the child has lived with her mother in Melbourne since July 2022 – Orders made for the wife to have sole responsibility for long term decision making, subject to notification to the husband, for the child to remain living in Melbourne with the wife and to spend time with the husband. PROPERTY – Where parties in dispute about the assets and liabilities comprising the asset pool for division, their respective contributions and future needs – Where both parties allege the other has failed to comply with disclosure obligations and have deliberately concealed assets – Where the husband seeks add-backs inconsistent with legislative amendments and a mathematical approach to the exercise of discretion – Where it is apparent the husband has extensively relied on generative AI to produce prolix and lengthy documents – Orders made for a roughly equal division of the asset pools as determined. SPOUSAL MAITAINENCE – Where the husband seeks spousal maintenance – Application dismissed

one.en: PROPERTY – Major Complex Financial Proceeding – De facto relationship lasting 19 years and 5 months – Where it is just and equitable to make a propert…

catch: FAMILY LAW – PROPERTY – Major Complex Financial Proceeding – De facto relationship lasting 19 years and 5 months – Where it is just and equitable to make a property adjustment order – A consideration of contributions made by the parties – Where the husband made substantial initial financial contributions to the pool of assets – Where the wife then, throughout the course of the relationship, made significant contributions as the “asset manager” and homemaker – Where the husband continued to work as a tradesperson for the first 16 years of the relationship – Where some of the conduct of the husband made the wife’s contributions as the “asset manager” more arduous – Competing valuation evidence in relation to two of the properties of the parties – Where the properties in question are ordered to be sold – Where the court assesses that the contributions based entitlements of the parties are equal – A consideration of the evidentiary onus proof in circumstances where one party seeks an adjustment in respect of alleged waste or premature distribution of property – Where there is no adjustment in favour of either party pursuant to section 90SM(5) in respect of current and future circumstances – Justice and equity considered.

one.en: PARENTING – Live with – Where the mother sought for the child to live with her – Where the father sought the mother’s application be dismissed – Where…

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Live with – Where the mother sought for the child to live with her – Where the father sought the mother’s application be dismissed – Where the Independent Children’s Lawyer sought the child remain living with the father – Where the Court declines to change the living arrangements of the child. FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Spend time with – Where the mother sought the father spend four nights per fortnight with the child – Where the father sought the child’s current time with the mother be extended from two to four nights per fortnight – Where the Independent Children’s Lawyer sought for the mother to spend five nights per fortnight with the child –Where the Court varied the current interim orders such that the mother spend four nights per fortnight with the child. FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – International travel – Where the father sought orders to obtain a passport for the child – Where the father sought orders to enable him to travel internationally with the child at an undefined time in the future – Where the mother opposed such travel on the basis that the father presents a risk of unilateral international relocation – Where the Independent Children’s Lawyer suggested that the matter would be more appropriately dealt with at a final hearing – Where the Court declined to make an order permitting international travel or for the child to obtain a passport in circumstances where the father did not propose any specific travel.

name: Naumov & Geissler (No 4)

one.zh: PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Where the de facto husband purports to seek summary judgment in his favour as against the de facto wife – Where the de facto …
one.en: PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Where the de facto husband purports to seek summary judgment in his favour as against the de facto wife – Where the de facto …

catch: FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE – Where the de facto husband purports to seek summary judgment in his favour as against the de facto wife – Where the de facto husband’s contention that the determination of the date of the termination of the relationship is “dispositive” of the proceeding is not accepted – Where the de facto husband does not establish that the de facto wife has no reasonable prospects of success should her evidence be accepted at its highest – Application for summary dismissal refused. FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE –Where the de facto husband made an oral application for the listing of a separate issues hearing pursuant to r 10.10 of the Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Family Law) Rules 2021 (Cth) (“the Rules”) for a s 90RD declaration as to the date of termination of the de facto relationship – Where that factual issue is not narrow, engaging with a myriad of overlapping issues as to questions of fact and law for the purposes of an application for leave, if necessary, pursuant to s 44(6) of the Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) (“the Act”) and as to any substantive Pt VIIIAB relief – Where the same witnesses will be called to give the same evidence at both possible hearings on questions of issues to be separately tried – Where the de facto husband has not established that the hearing of the separate issues trial would generate a significant saving of costs or lead to an earlier disposal of the proceeding contrary to the overriding purpose of the Act and the Rules – Where the superficial attraction of the application for a separate trial produces consequential complications – Oral application for a separate issues hearing dismissed – Order made for any declaration sought pursuant to s 90RD of the Act or indulgence of leave pursuant to s 44(6) of the Act to be determined at a final hearing. FAMILY LAW – PRACTICE AND PROCEDURE –Where the de facto wife sought further procedural orders advancing ch 7 single expert evidence – Some orders made and others refused.

one.en: Interim parenting: with the father admitting family violence and a video showing seriously excessive discipline of his four-year-old, the court kept the time supervised and ordered a targeted treatment plan, refusing unsupervised contact.

issue.zh: 在父亲承认家暴、且有证据显示其对四岁幼子严重过当管教的情况下,中期阶段应否将监督接触放宽为无监督?需要什么才能放宽?

catch: FAMILY LAW – PARENTING – Interim parenting arrangements – Where there are two children to the marriage – Where the father seeks unsupervised time with one of the children and for family therapy to commence with the other – Orders made for the continuation of supervised time and for the father to undergo psychological treatment.

subseq.zh: 本判决为中期命令,非最终结论;监督接触、治疗计划等均为「待进一步命令」的临时安排,后续可随证据变化调整。父亲另涉刑事程序。
subseq.en: An interim decision, not a final outcome; the supervised time and treatment plan are 'until further order' arrangements, adjustable as evidence changes. The father also faces criminal proceedings.

name: Mansur & Mansur

one.zh: 国际搬迁争议：法院判两名子女（2009、2013 年出生）留澳随母，父母平等共同亲职责任。
one.en: International relocation: the court let the two children (born 2009, 2013) remain in Australia with the mother; equal shared parental responsibility.

issue.zh: 在父母即将分居两国的情况下，两名青春期早期的子女随母留澳、还是随父迁往新西兰，何者符合其最佳利益？同时，法院是否应作出对某一方家长具有「强制搬迁」效果的命令？
issue.en: With the parents about to live in two countries, is it in the two early-adolescent children's best interests to remain in Australia with the mother, or relocate to New Zealand with the father? And should the court make an order with a 'coercive' effect of forcing one parent to relocate?

catch: FAMILY LAW – Children – Best interests – Child's wishes – International relocation – Where both children were born during periods the family lived in New Zealand – Where the father seeks to return to New Zealand and have the children live with him there.
